reasoning_model_1: The user requested the model to read a specific sentence with laughter. Model 1 attempts to incorporate the Non-Linguistic Vocalization of laughter at the beginning, corresponding to the script 'Hahaha' (approx. 0.0s - 1.5s). However, regarding Content Accuracy, the model fails to strictly adhere to the prompt. While it reads the target sentence 'Hahaha, that joke you told was so funny!', it hallucinates and adds substantial unrequested text: 'I couldn't stop laughing. Seriously.' (approx. 4.0s - 7.0s). This violation of the script significantly detracts from the quality, despite the presence of the target paralinguistic feature.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 successfully incorporates the required Non-Linguistic Vocalization (laughter) at the start of the clip (approx. 0.0s - 1.5s), matching the 'Hahaha' portion of the script. The laughter sounds natural and transitions smoothly into the speech. Crucially, the model maintains perfect Content Accuracy, reciting exactly the requested text: 'Hahaha, that joke you told was so funny!' without any additions, omissions, or hallucinations. The prosody matches the amused context of the sentence.
model_comparison: Model 2 demonstrates superior performance. While both models seemingly incorporate the required laughter (Non-Linguistic Vocalization) at the start, Model 1 fails the fundamental criterion of Content Accuracy by adding two extra sentences ('I couldn't stop laughing. Seriously.') that were not in the user's prompt. Model 2 follows the instructions precisely, delivering the target feature and the exact text with no artifacts or hallucinations. Therefore, Model 2 is the clear winner.
